data.title: Asian Markets Expected to Open Weak Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty
data.description: Asian stock markets are anticipated to open weak on Tuesday, April 28, as geopolitical uncertainties persist. According to Jin10, investors are shifting their focus to the earnings reports of major U.S. tech giants, which collectively have a market capitalization of nearly $16 trillion (16万亿美元). Walter Todd, President and Chief Investment Officer of Greenwood Capital Associates, noted that the U.S. stock market has rebounded to historical highs, seemingly disregarding ongoing geopolitical risks. This resilience is largely attributed to upward revisions in earnings expectations and improved market sentiment. However, Todd cautioned that any cracks in this optimistic outlook, as companies release their earnings in the coming weeks, could pose significant risks to market trends, potentially hindering the rally of large tech stocks.

data.title: Colorado and xAI Seek Court Delay on AI Law Challenge
data.description: Colorado and xAI have jointly requested a federal court to pause deadlines in xAI's legal challenge against SB24-205. According to NS3.AI, this move comes as lawmakers are considering amendments to the state's AI legislation. Colorado Attorney General Phil Weiser announced that the state will refrain from enforcing SB24-205 or any new law from this session until the legislative and rulemaking processes are finalized.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | SPDR Gold Trust Holdings Decrease
data.description: The world's largest gold ETF, SPDR Gold Trust, reported a reduction in its holdings by 2.285 tons, bringing the current total to 1,044.337 tons. According to Jin10, this adjustment reflects recent market activities and investor sentiment towards gold.

data.title: CME Reports 100% Probability of Fed Holding Rates Steady in April
data.description: On April 28, Jin10 reported that according to CME's 'FedWatch', there is a 100% probability that the U.S. Federal Reserve will maintain its interest rates unchanged in April. Furthermore, the probability of the Fed cumulatively cutting rates by 25 basis points by June stands at 4.5%, while the likelihood of keeping rates steady is at 95.5%.

data.title: ZetaChain Halts Cross-Chain Transactions Following GatewayEVM Contract Attack
data.description: ZetaChain has temporarily suspended cross-chain transactions after an attack on its GatewayEVM contract. According to NS3.AI, the company reported that the incident was limited to internal team wallets and did not compromise user funds. ZetaChain assured that the attack vector has been contained, and an investigation is ongoing with a post-mortem analysis planned.

data.title: Bitcoin 2026 Conference Faces Criticism for Straying from Anti-Establishment Roots
data.description: Bitcoin 2026 kicked off on Monday in Las Vegas, drawing criticism from early adopters who argue that the event has deviated from Bitcoin's original anti-establishment ethos. According to NS3.AI, the conference, organized by BTC Inc., is anticipated to attract over 40,000 attendees and feature 500 speakers by Wednesday at The Venetian.

data.title: DOJ's New Stance on Software Developers in Criminal Cases
data.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche announced that software developers who do not assist third parties in committing crimes will not face investigation or charges from the Department of Justice (DOJ). According to NS3.AI, Blanche emphasized that the department has significantly altered its approach towards developers, even as it proceeds with ongoing cases involving Tornado Cash and Samourai Wallet.

data.title: Avi Eisenberg Denies Threatening Aave Following Wallet Signature Link
data.description: Avi Eisenberg has refuted claims that he threatened to attack Aave after Arkham Intelligence connected a new wallet signature to him. Eisenberg, who was previously convicted for the $110 million exploit of Mango Markets, stated that he had privately informed the Aave team of a potential risk in 2022, rather than targeting the protocol. According to NS3.AI, Eisenberg emphasized that his actions were aimed at alerting the team to vulnerabilities rather than posing a direct threat.

data.title: Tennessee to Ban Cryptocurrency Kiosks Starting July 1
data.description: Tennessee will prohibit the use and installation of cryptocurrency kiosks beginning July 1. According to NS3.AI, Governor Bill Lee has signed legislation that categorizes the installation of these kiosks as a Class A misdemeanor. Businesses and operators involved in setting up the kiosks could face penalties of up to 11 months and 29 days in prison, along with a $2,500 fine.

data.title: Wind Developers Shift Focus to Fossil Fuels Under Trump Administration Agreements
data.description: Two offshore wind developers have decided to abandon their federal leases in favor of investing in fossil fuel projects, according to the Interior Department's announcement on Monday. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the shift in strategy as part of agreements with the Trump administration. This move marks a significant change in the energy sector, as developers redirect their focus from renewable energy to traditional fossil fuel investments. The decision comes amid ongoing discussions about energy policies and the balance between renewable and non-renewable energy sources.

data.title: Kalshi Launches Cryptocurrency Perpetual Futures with USD Collateral
data.description: Kalshi is introducing cryptocurrency perpetual futures today, utilizing U.S. dollars as the initial collateral. According to NS3.AI, John Wang, Kalshi's head of crypto, highlighted that Bitcoin serves as the primary source of user payments within Kalshi's applications. The platform intends to incorporate stablecoins in the future.

data.title: Cantor Fitzgerald Analyst Raises Price Targets for SanDisk and Western Digital Amid AI Demand
data.description: Cantor Fitzgerald analyst C.J. Muse has increased the price targets for SanDisk and Western Digital, reflecting optimism in the tech sector driven by artificial intelligence demand. According to NS3.AI, Muse raised SanDisk's price target from $1,000 to $1,400 and Western Digital's from $420 to $500. He maintained Overweight ratings for both stocks, citing strong AI demand, tight supply conditions, and an improving earnings outlook as key factors influencing his decision.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Reports Significant Bond Purchases Ahead of Fed Decision
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has disclosed bond purchases amounting to as much as $161 million, according to a Periodic Transaction Report released by the U.S. Office of Government Ethics. According to NS3.AI, the report, which details transactions made in March 2026, lists 175 transactions with bond purchases totaling at least $51 million at the lower end of the reported value ranges. This disclosure comes just days before the Federal Open Market Committee is set to announce its rate decision on Wednesday at 2 p.m. Eastern.

data.title: Taylor Swift Files Trademark Applications for Voice and Likeness
data.description: Taylor Swift's company has submitted three trademark applications in the United States related to her voice and likeness. According to NS3.AI, these applications include two sound marks and one stage image. Kirk Sigmon noted that enforcing these trademarks against anonymous misuse by AI online could still pose challenges.

data.title: NFT Market Cap Surpasses $2 Billion Amid Strong Performance
data.description: The global NFT market cap exceeded $2 billion over the weekend, marking a significant milestone in its recent growth trajectory. According to NS3.AI, the market cap stood at $1.87 billion following its most robust 30-day performance in months. Yuga Labs' collections played a pivotal role in driving this upward trend, while CryptoPunks maintained its position as the largest collection by market cap.

data.title: Brazil's Finance Minister Expects Continued Interest Rate Cuts
data.description: Brazil's Finance Minister has indicated that the country is expected to continue with interest rate cuts. According to Jin10, this statement reflects the government's ongoing efforts to stimulate economic growth and manage inflation. The minister's comments come amid a broader trend of monetary easing in the region, as countries seek to bolster their economies in the face of global uncertainties.

data.title: SEC Chair Paul Atkins: Cryptocurrency and Blockchain Innovation to Strengthen U.S. Economy
data.description: SEC Chair Paul Atkins has expressed optimism about the role of cryptocurrency and blockchain technology in enhancing the U.S. economy and financial system. According to NS3.AI, Atkins believes that these innovations will provide significant benefits, potentially leading to increased efficiency and security within financial markets. His comments come amid ongoing discussions about the regulatory landscape for digital assets, highlighting the importance of fostering innovation while ensuring adequate oversight. Atkins' stance reflects a growing recognition of the transformative potential of blockchain technology in various sectors.

data.title: U.S. Senator Cynthia Lummis Anticipates Finalization of CLARITY Crypto Bill in May
data.description: U.S. Senator Cynthia Lummis has expressed her expectation that the CLARITY crypto market structure bill will be finalized in May. According to NS3.AI, this legislative effort aims to establish a comprehensive framework for the regulation of cryptocurrency markets in the United States. The bill is designed to address various aspects of the crypto industry, including market transparency, investor protection, and regulatory oversight. Senator Lummis, a known advocate for digital assets, believes that the bill will provide much-needed clarity and stability to the rapidly evolving crypto sector. The finalization of the CLARITY bill is anticipated to have significant implications for both investors and regulatory bodies, potentially shaping the future of cryptocurrency regulation in the country.

data.title: U.S. Representative to Reintroduce Bitcoin Reserve Bill with New Name
data.description: U.S. Representative Nick Begich is set to reintroduce the Bitcoin Strategic Reserve Bill in the coming weeks, now renamed as the American Reserve Modernization Act (ARMA). According to NS3.AI, Begich stated that the new name aims to expand support in Congress and position Bitcoin as a reserve asset.

data.title: Regulatory Reset for U.S. Digital Assets Signaled by SEC and CFTC Chairs
data.description: SEC Chair Paul Atkins and CFTC Chair Mike Selig have indicated a shift in regulatory approach for U.S. digital assets during the Bitcoin 2026 event. According to NS3.AI, Atkins emphasized the SEC's intention to encourage crypto activities within the U.S. and announced an upcoming initiative for supervised testing of tokenized securities on-chain. Selig highlighted the necessity for a coordinated framework between the SEC and CFTC to address products that exhibit both commodity-like and security-like characteristics.
